# Adi Shalev - Chinese Medicine Clinic

> Adi Shalev is a certified Chinese medicine practitioner with 8+ years of experience, based in Rishon LeZion, Israel. The clinic specializes in acupuncture (Master Tung style), shiatsu, cupping therapy, and Chinese herbal medicine.

## About

- Name: Adi Shalev (עדי שלו)
- Profession: Chinese Medicine Practitioner
- Location: West Rishon LeZion, Israel
- Languages: Hebrew
- Experience: 8+ years
- Website: https://adishalev.co.il
- Contact: WhatsApp +972-52-802-9031

## Services

- **Acupuncture (דיקור סיני)**: Traditional Chinese acupuncture using Master Tung's method. Treats pain, migraines, anxiety, digestive issues, sleep disorders, and more. [Learn more](https://adishalev.co.il/dikur-sini)
- **Shiatsu (שיאצו)**: Japanese pressure therapy based on Chinese medicine meridians. No needles. [Learn more](https://adishalev.co.il/shiatsu)
- **Cupping Therapy (כוסות רוח)**: Traditional cupping for muscle tension, pain relief, and circulation. [Learn more](https://adishalev.co.il/cupping)
- **Chinese Herbal Medicine (צמחי מרפא סיניים)**: Personalized herbal formulas for internal conditions. [Learn more](https://adishalev.co.il/herbs)

## Conditions Treated

- Back pain, neck pain, shoulder pain, sciatica
- Migraines and headaches
- Anxiety and stress
- Sleep disorders and insomnia
- Digestive issues (IBS, reflux, bloating)
- Women's health (menstrual issues, fertility support)
- Sports injuries and rehabilitation

## FAQ

Q: Does acupuncture hurt?
A: Usually not. The needles are extremely thin and most patients feel only a slight tingling or heaviness - not pain. Many even fall asleep during treatment.

Q: How many treatments are needed?
A: It depends on the condition. Acute issues (like sudden back pain) can improve within 3-5 sessions. Chronic conditions usually require a longer series. A personalized plan is created at the first visit.

Q: What's the difference between acupuncture and shiatsu?
A: Acupuncture uses thin needles along meridian points. Shiatsu uses manual pressure on the same points - no needles. Both are based on the same Chinese medicine principles.

Q: What to expect at the first visit?
A: The first session includes a comprehensive conversation about your condition, medical history, and lifestyle, followed by treatment - about 60 minutes total. Come neither hungry nor full.

## Credentials

- Certified Chinese Medicine Practitioner
- Specializes in Master Tung acupuncture style
- 8+ years of clinical experience
- Located in Rishon LeZion, central Israel
